verses.text: By Babylon's rivers the exiles wept for Zion, hanging up their harps, while their captors mocked, 'Sing us one of the songs of Zion.' Grief silenced their music. There are sorrows too deep for the songs the world demands.

By Babylon's rivers the exiles wept for Zion, hanging up their harps, while their captors mocked, 'Sing us one of the songs of Zion.' Grief silenced their music. There are sorrows too deep for the songs the world demands.

By Babylon's rivers the exiles wept for Zion, hanging up their harps, while their captors mocked, 'Sing us one of the songs of Zion.' Grief silenced their music. There are sorrows too deep for the songs the world demands.

'How shall we sing the LORD's song in a strange land?' they ask, vowing, 'If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget her cunning.' Love for Zion held firm. The faithful would sooner lose all skill and joy than forget the city of God.

'How shall we sing the LORD's song in a strange land?' they ask, vowing, 'If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget her cunning.' Love for Zion held firm. The faithful would sooner lose all skill and joy than forget the city of God.

'How shall we sing the LORD's song in a strange land?' they ask, vowing, 'If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget her cunning.' Love for Zion held firm. The faithful would sooner lose all skill and joy than forget the city of God.

They call God to remember Edom's cruelty and pronounce woe on Babylon for her violence. The cry for justice is fierce. Such words commit the wrongs of ruthless enemies to the righteous judgment of God.
